title: olea europaea subsp. europaea
intro: Olea europaea subsp. europaea, commonly known as the European olive tree, is a subspecies of the olive tree that is native to the Mediterranean region. This plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The European olive tree has been found to have various health benefits, including reducing the risk of heart disease, improving cognitive function, and aiding in weight management. The leaves, bark, and oil of the European olive tree are commonly used in herbal preparations, such as tea, tinctures, and capsules, to support overall health and well-being.
